title: Prioritize Arrivals Over Departures During Slot Allocation
description: The system shall prioritize arriving flights over departing flights during slot allocation to reduce holding patterns and improve landing efficiency.
rationale: This requirement addresses the need to manage airspace more efficiently by ensuring that arrivals are given priority in slot conflicts, thereby reducing delays and improving overall air traffic flow.
business_value: Reducing holding patterns and improving landing efficiency can lead to increased runway capacity, reduced fuel consumption for aircraft, and enhanced customer satisfaction.

source.description: As a air traffic controller I need arrivals to be prioritized over departures during slot allocation So that holding patterns are reduced and landing efficiency is improved

acceptance_criteria: Given there is a slot conflict between an arriving flight and a departing flight
When the slot allocation algorithm runs
Then the arriving flight is assigned the conflicting slot
And the departing flight is assigned an alternative slot
preconditions: A set of arrival and departure requests for a given time window
postconditions: All arrival slots are placed before any departure slots are adjusted
functional_constraints: Arriving flights must be considered higher priority than departing flights when slot conflicts occur
Departure slots should only be adjusted after arrival slots are finalized

non_functional_requirements.performance: The system should handle high volumes of flight requests without significant delays in slot allocation
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available 99.9% of the time to minimize disruptions
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system should be able to scale to accommodate additional airports or airspaces with increasing traffic
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error handling mechanisms should provide clear and concise messages to users

edge_cases: What happens when there are multiple conflicting slots for arrivals and departures?
How does the system handle a sudden surge in traffic that exceeds capacity?
What if an arriving flight is delayed unexpectedly during slot allocation?
dependencies: The existing slot allocation algorithm must be updated to support priority logic.
All arrival requests must be processed before departure slots are finalized.

interfaces.name: Flight Request API
interfaces.description: The system will interact with this API to receive real-time flight request data and provide updated slot allocations.

interfaces.name: Slot Allocation Database
interfaces.description: This database will store the current state of slot allocations, arrival/departure requests, and priority information.

test_cases.description: Test scenario where there is a single conflict between an arriving and a departing flight.
test_cases.expected_result: The system should assign the conflicting slot to the arriving flight and provide an alternative slot for the departing flight.

test_cases.description: Test scenario with multiple conflicts where both arrivals and departures are affected.
test_cases.expected_result: Arrival slots should be prioritized over departure slots, and all arrival requests should be placed before any departure slots are adjusted.

test_cases.description: Test scenario with a sudden surge in arrival requests that exceed available slots.
test_cases.expected_result: The system should dynamically adjust slot allocations to accommodate the increased demand while maintaining priority for arrivals.
